sources.name: Tamil Nadu Assembly Election 2026 — alliance coverage

parties.notes: Sentinel for non-party candidates. All independent candidates collapse to this id; their per-contest distinctiveness is preserved via candidate-level entity_ids.

Sentinel for ECI party_shorts not yet mapped in this roster. Collapses many distinct minor/regional parties — party-* rollups for UNK are NOT meaningful and must not be surfaced in citizen UI. Backfill drivers use this as fallback to keep candidate-* + ac-* observations complete. Triage by promoting verified entries (with eci_code) to dedicated party_id rows.
Introduced by ECI in 2013 (PUCL v. Union of India). Pre-2013 nota_* indicators are null, not zero.
National party. Successor to Bharatiya Jana Sangh via the Janata Party interregnum.
National party. Oldest major political party in India.

parties.full_name: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam
aliases: All India Anna Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam
parties.notes: Split from DMK in 1972 by M. G. Ramachandran.
Renamed from TRS to BRS in 2022.
2022 split of Shiv Sena; Election Commission allotted the Shiv Sena name+symbol to the Eknath Shinde faction. The Uddhav-led faction is registered under this name.
2023 split of NCP; ECI allotted the NCP name+symbol to the Ajit Pawar faction.

parties.full_name: Communist Marxist Party Kerala State Committee

parties.notes: Registered unrecognised; active across Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu.
Recognised state party in UP; founded by Ajit Singh.
Tamil Nadu party founded by Kamal Haasan in 2018.
Founded by Ram Vilas Paswan in 2000. Split into LJP(RV) and RLJP in 2021; pre-split rows use LJP.
Marxist-Leninist communist party; long-tail contestants across many states.
Simranjit Singh Mann's faction of SAD.
Recognised state party in Haryana; founded by Dushyant Chautala in 2018.
Bihar party founded by Upendra Kushwaha in 2013; later merged into JD(U).
UP-based; founded by Chandrashekhar Azad in 2020.

parties.full_name: Nirbal Indian Shoshit Hamara Aam Dal

parties.notes: Jharkhand party; merged into BJP in 2020.
Multi-state party; active in Kerala, Karnataka, UP, etc.
Bihar party founded by Pappu Yadav.
Long-tail multi-state contestant; tentative full-name resolution.
Tribal rights party in Gujarat and Rajasthan.
Variant short for AJSU (state party in Jharkhand).
Tamil Nadu regional party representing Kongu Vellala Gounder community.

parties.full_name: All India Puratchi Thalaivar Makkal Munnettra Kazhagam

parties.notes: UP party founded by Om Prakash Rajbhar in 2002.
Tamil Nadu party founded by K. Krishnasamy.
Karnataka regional party founded by Ravi Krishna Reddy.
